commit.message:
feat: narrow scatter cone (0–10 tiles) + live cone overlay on grid

Accuracy values rescaled to 0–10 tile range:
  laspistol 2, lasgun 3, bolt_pistol 3, autogun 4, assault_rifle 5, lmg 10

Live cone visualization on Phaser grid during targeting:
  - MissionSystem emits 'shot-mode' { active, fromPos, accuracy } whenever
    shootingMode or fireMode changes
  - CombatScene listens for 'shot-mode' and registers a pointermove handler
  - _drawCone() draws a filled amber triangle (apex at shooter, base at cursor,
    half-width = accuracy tiles) plus a bright shot-line tracer
  - Single-shot (accuracy=0) shows only the tracer line, no cone
  - Cone clears automatically when shooting mode is cancelled

commit.message:
Fix event timing: use actual game date from ticker, not Kalshi's 2-week window

Kalshi's close_time is the outer settlement deadline (often 2+ weeks out),
not when the game actually happens. Now:
- Extract event date from ticker (e.g. 26MAR09 = March 9, 2026)
- Use event date for urgency scoring (today's games rank highest)
- Store event date as close_time on trades instead of Kalshi's window
- Show AI the actual hours_until_event, not hours_until_close

commit.message:
Fix SSM SecureString CloudFormation compatibility

Replace CDK SSM construct reference with a direct IAM policy grant.
CloudFormation cannot resolve SecureString parameters at deploy time,
but the Lambda can fetch them at runtime via the SDK with proper IAM perms.

commit.message:
fix: persist capturing indicator until wave 2 text arrives; fix paragraph break in saved message

Two bugs: (1) tool_start+input could arrive in same SSE chunk, so
isCapturingInput flipped true→false before React rendered — indicator
was invisible. Fix: don't clear isCapturingInput on input; clear it
when the first wave-2 text-delta arrives instead. (2) The server saved
fullText without the \n\n, so the paragraph break disappeared when the
complete event replaced the streaming message. Fix: inject \n\n into
fullText at the tool-call boundary on the server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: replace Gmail OAuth2 with IMAP/SMTP sidecar proxy

Replace the OAuth2 token refresh approach (which required re-auth every
7 days) with an IMAP/SMTP sidecar that uses a permanent App Password.
The sidecar runs in the credential-proxy container, keeps the password
off the agent, and exposes Gmail REST API-compatible HTTP endpoints so
GmailProvider needs no changes.

Also includes container hardening: non-root users, gosu/su-exec privilege
dropping, and capability restrictions across all three containers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make PQ PKI init non-fatal due to NSS ML-DSA-87 chain verify limitation

NSS 3.119 can create ML-DSA-87 keys and certificates but cannot verify
ML-DSA-87 signatures in certificate chain validation (error -8016).
Root CA initializes but subordinate CAs fail TLS handshake because the
TLS cert chain includes ML-DSA-87 signatures that NSS can't validate.

Mark PQ PKI as experimental and allow partial initialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: cert-revocation-lab
repository.description: Event-driven certificate revocation lab with triple PKI hierarchies (RSA-4096, ECC P-384, ML-DSA-87). Automated Zero Trust response using Dogtag PKI, FreeIPA, Kafka, and Event-Driven Ansible.

commit.message:
fix: replace string-based detection with symbol-based detection (#1030)

## Summary
- Remove string-based `IsActionDelegate`/`IsEventHandlerDelegate`
fallbacks in `EventSyntaxExtensions`. All delegate type checks now
require `KnownSymbols` and use symbol comparison via
`NamedTypeSymbolExtensions`.
- Replace `string.Equals(targetMethod.Name, "Of")` in
`LinqToMocksExpressionShouldBeValidAnalyzer` with
`targetMethod.IsInstanceOf(knownSymbols.MockOf)` for proper symbol-based
detection.
- Hoist `ReferencedAssemblyNames` string scan in
`SetupShouldNotIncludeAsyncResultAnalyzer` from per-node `Analyze` into
`RegisterCompilationStartAction`, performing the version check once per
compilation instead of per syntax node.
- All three analyzers now follow the established pattern:
`RegisterCompilationStartAction` + `IsMockReferenced()` guard +
closure-captured `MoqKnownSymbols`.
- Remove duplicate `WithKnownSymbols` test variants that became
identical after consolidation.

## Test plan
- [x] All 2894 existing tests pass
- [x] Build succeeds with zero warnings and zero errors
- [x] `EventSyntaxExtensions` tests updated to use `KnownSymbols`
parameter
- [x] Duplicate test methods removed to resolve S4144 warnings
- [x] Verified no remaining string-based type detection in analyzer
source files

Closes #981

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Corrected Mock.Of invocation validation detection
* Improved Raises/RaisesAsync event-argument validation to reduce false
positives

* **Refactor**
* Simplified analyzer validation flows and consolidated event-handling
helpers
  * Added early-name checks to optimize Raises detection performance

* **Tests**
* Expanded and data-driven unit tests for event/delegate scenarios and
result-property checks

* **Chores**
  * Added THIRD-PARTY-NOTICES.TXT to package baseline
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Richard Murillo <rjmurillo@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor Mot from interface to abstract class

Mot is now an abstract class with three subclasses (MotValue, MotRef,
MotUndefined). Shared read logic (path dispatch, array extraction, has)
lives in the base class; subclasses implement only leaf-level behavior.

MotValue is exported and generic (MotValue<M>) so external wrappers
like Tag can extend it without reimplementing read methods.

Added isRef, MotRefData, and createRefMot to the factory interface so
buildMot preserves reference structure for consumers that need it.

commit.message:
fix: correct resource rates, build progress bar, and eager build processing

- Remove double speed-multiplier in client interpolation (rates already include it)
- Add shared updatePlanetResources utility for API routes
- All API endpoints (buildings, research, shipyard, game state) now update
  resources and recalculate production rates before returning data
- Client hydrates resources from every API response (not just WS sync)
- Build progress bar now uses actual startedAt/completesAt instead of
  assuming 1-hour builds
- BuildQueueTimer retries polling every 3s when expired (not single shot)

  token smuggling); hard-blocks CSAM/synthesis requests; integrated
  into FirebaseAIService.generateText() as first-pass sanitizer

  org membership gating, anonymous user commercial op restriction

  nodeIntegration=false, sandbox=true; preload uses contextBridge
- Item 256: docs/KEY_ROTATION_RUNBOOK.md — step-by-step rotation
  for Firebase, Gemini, Stripe, Service Account, PandaDoc, Pinata, WC
- Item 258: LegalAuditService prevHash SHA-256 chain — each entry

feat(sdk): add AuthClient, NotificationClient, and JWTAuthMiddleware (#213, #215, #216) (#224)

Consolidate cross-app duplicated capabilities into the SDK:

- AuthClient: async wrapper for isA_user auth_service (token verification,
  API key management, token generation). Replaces raw HTTP calls in a2a.py
  and graph_registry_with_auth.py.

- NotificationClient: async wrapper for isA_user notification_service
  (send, batch, in-app, templates, queries). Replaces Trade's custom
  343-line notification dispatcher.

- JWTAuthMiddleware: ASGI middleware supporting both auth_service delegation
  and local JWT validation. Replaces Creative's custom auth.py that was
  copy-pasted across apps.

- DomainEvent compatibility tests verifying cross-app event serialization
  roundtrips work correctly (supports #216 Marketing migration).

69 new tests (L1 unit + L2 component), all passing.
Full unit suite regression: 1282 passed.

Fixes #213, Fixes #215
Related to #216, #211

commit.message:
Add automatic column sync from publisher to subscriber

Periodically compare information_schema.columns between publisher and
subscriber for all published tables. Missing columns on subscriber are
automatically added via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with correct type and default.

Runs every 30s in the existing ddl_sync_loop alongside table sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: support SimpleLambdaExpressionSyntax in callback validation (#1042)

## Summary

Closes #1012.

- Widens `TryGetCallbackLambda` return type from
`ParenthesizedLambdaExpressionSyntax?` to `LambdaExpressionSyntax?`,
removing the explicit bail-out for `SimpleLambdaExpressionSyntax` in
delegate constructors
- Adds `GetLambdaParameters` helper to uniformly extract parameters from
both `ParenthesizedLambdaExpressionSyntax` and
`SimpleLambdaExpressionSyntax`
- Adds `GetDiagnosticNode` helper for correct diagnostic span placement
on each lambda form
- Adds test cases for simple lambdas in delegate constructors (both
valid and invalid patterns)

## Test plan

- [x] All 2917 existing tests pass with zero failures
- [x] New valid patterns: `new Action<string>(x => { })` and `new
Func<string, int>(x => 42)` produce no diagnostic
- [x] New invalid patterns: `new Action<int>(x => { })` (wrong type) and
`new Action<int>(x => { })` (argument count mismatch) produce Moq1100
- [x] `dotnet build` succeeds with zero warnings

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
* Broader support for simple lambdas and delegate-wrapped callbacks in
validation and diagnostics.
* Improved fixer: converts simple lambdas to parenthesized form,
rebuilds parameter lists from the mocked method, preserves formatting,
and skips unsafe fixes inside delegate-constructor contexts.
  * Better symbol resolution fallback when overload resolution fails.

* **Tests**
* Added/extended tests for simple-lambda callbacks, delegate-wrapped
callbacks, and new fixer scenarios; updated expected parameter renames.

* **Chores**
  * Test helper overload for configurable code-fix iteration counts.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
Fix ThreadPoolExecutor blocking on shutdown after timeout

shutdown(wait=True) blocks forever if workers are stuck on I/O.
Use manual executor management: shutdown(wait=False) when timeout
occurred, shutdown(wait=True) for normal completion.
